stimulus: The Royal Tyrrell Museum in Canada and the Museo Egizio in Italy are two of many institutions around the world devoted to curating extensive collections of fragile ______blank training the next generation of specialized conservators; and engaging the public through traveling exhibitions on cultural heritage.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is the punctuation of items in a complex series with internal punctuation. The semicolon after "recent" separates the first item ("curating...recent") from the second ("training...conservators") in a series of activities. The comma after "artifacts" correctly separates the noun phrase "extensive collections of fragile artifacts" from the supplementary phrase "both ancient and recent" that modifies it.

Choice A is incorrect because a comma cannot separate the first and second items of a complex series with internal punctuation. Choice B is incorrect because it fails to separate the noun phrase from its supplementary phrase and uses a comma in place of the required semicolon. Choice C is incorrect because a semicolon cannot separate the noun phrase from its supplementary phrase, and a comma cannot separate complex series items.
